short_text: NGN/USD exchange rate as ML feature
full_text: The NGN/USD exchange rate should be included as a predictive feature in the iNHCES model

short_text: CPI inflation rate as ML feature
full_text: The CPI/inflation rate (annual %) should be included as a predictive feature in the model

short_text: Brent crude oil price as ML feature
full_text: Brent crude oil price (USD/barrel) should be included as a predictive feature in the model

short_text: GDP growth rate as ML feature
full_text: Nigeria's GDP growth rate (annual %) should be included as a predictive feature
The commercial lending interest rate should be included as a predictive feature
The iNHCES system should use live, automatically updated macroeconomic data, not static historical tables

short_text: Weekly or more frequent macro data refresh
full_text: Macroeconomic features in the model should be refreshed at least once per week automatically
Gross floor area in square metres (sqm) should be a mandatory input feature for every estimate
Number of building storeys (above ground) should be a mandatory input feature
Structural system type (masonry/reinforced concrete frame/steel frame) should be a mandatory input feature
Project location expressed as geopolitical zone or state should be a mandatory input feature
Project type (purely residential / commercial / mixed-use) should be a mandatory input feature
Procurement method (direct labour / traditional / design-and-build) should be included as an input feature
Specification level (standard / medium / luxury finish) should be included as an input feature
Number of residential units or bedrooms should be included as an input feature
The deployed iNHCES model should achieve a Mean Absolute Percentage Error (MAPE) of <= 15% on held-out validation data
The model must provide SHAP-based feature importance explanations for every estimate generated
The model should provide 90% prediction intervals alongside point estimates to communicate uncertainty
The model should be automatically retrained at least quarterly as new project cost data becomes available

short_text: Benchmark multiple ML algorithms before deployment
full_text: Multiple ML algorithms (e.g. Ridge, RF, XGBoost, LightGBM, MLP) should be benchmarked before the champion model is selected for deployment

short_text: Stacking ensemble as champion if it outperforms
full_text: A stacking ensemble should be selected as the champion model if it outperforms individual models on cross-validated MAPE
Cost estimate generation (model inference + SHAP calculation) should complete within 3 seconds
The system should support at least 50 simultaneous active users without performance degradation
The iNHCES web system should maintain at least 99.5% uptime (scheduled maintenance excluded)

short_text: Full audit trail for all estimates
full_text: Every estimate should be stored with a full audit trail: user identity, timestamp, all inputs, model version, and outputs

short_text: Auto-generate PDF report for every estimate
full_text: The system should automatically generate a professionally formatted PDF cost report for every estimate completed
The user interface should be fully accessible via standard web browser without any software installation
A qualified QS professional should be able to complete the estimate input form in under 5 minutes

short_text: Visual cost breakdown chart in results
full_text: Estimate results should include a visual cost breakdown chart (elemental or trade-by-trade)

short_text: Data freshness indicators for macro variables
full_text: The interface should display data freshness indicators showing the age of each macroeconomic data source
The user interface should be fully usable on mobile phones and tablet devices

short_text: Multiple saved project profiles per user
full_text: Each registered user should be able to save and manage multiple project profiles
The system should display a warning flag when any macroeconomic data source is more than 7 days old

short_text: DATA SOURCE confidence classification on estimates
full_text: Each estimate should display a DATA SOURCE confidence classification (GREEN/AMBER/RED) indicating data quality
Missing or invalid input values should produce clear, user-friendly validation messages — not system crashes
All numeric inputs should be validated against plausible real-world range limits before submission

short_text: NIQS cost plan format in reports
full_text: Generated PDF reports should follow the NIQS (Nigerian Institute of Quantity Surveyors) cost plan format
Reports should include a sensitivity analysis showing estimate variation under 3 exchange rate scenarios (base, +15%, -15%)
Cost reports should be exportable as professionally formatted, branded PDF documents

short_text: Report format acceptable for tender submission
full_text: The report format should be acceptable for submission alongside tender documents in Nigerian public procurement
